title: OpenCode Model Routing — Fallback Chain Contract
description: Canonical schema for the per-agent fallback chain field consumed by both the Go writer (omp) and the TypeScript runtime plugin. The chain lives at `agent.<name>.options.fallback_models` inside OpenCode's global `opencode.json`. The `options` slot is OpenCode's documented extension surface — `AgentConfig.normalize()` in packages/opencode/src/config/agent.ts relocates any non-allow-listed sibling key into `options` at config load time, so writing directly to `options.fallback_models` matches the schema contract rather than relying on the transform side-effect. See design.md § D1 for the source citation.

fallback_models.description: Ordered list of `provider/model` pairs to try in sequence when the primary model fails. Empty array == no fallback (single-model behavior).

$comment: Cross-stack consumers MUST reference the field name `fallback_models` verbatim. Drift between Go writer (internal/config/) and TS reader (plugin/src/) is enforced via schema-contract-check.sh in `make lint`.
